turns.speaker: P
turns.text: um , the patient has high blood sugar . yeah , osteoarthritis , arterial fibrillation , and reflux .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: the doctor is good . is Hannah ready to get started ?

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: so , hannah is a , uh , 52-year-old female here for evaluation of a high blood sugar . so , hannah , what ha- what happened ? the doctor heard Hannah was in the emergency room with a high blood sugar .

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: yes . so , Hannah has been eating a ton of sweets recently . with the holidays the holidays just feels like there is always something sweet available. whether Hannah is at work and there are holiday gift baskets or clients bringing in treats or when Hannah visits Hannah's family for the holidays and there is every baked good imaginable, there is always something sweet Hannah can be eating. in the beginning Hannah felt fine, but Hannah has started to feel the effects of eating a ton of sweets after a couple of weeks, and this past week Hannah just felt really light-headed and Hannah could ... was seeing spots , really dizzy . so Hannah went into the emergency room and the emergency room staff said Hannah had a high blood sugar . um , so Hannah wanted to come in and follow up with the doctor to get the high blood sugar , um , get checked out .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay . all right . and how , how is Hannah feeling now ? did the emergency room staff , did , did the emergency room staff treat Hannah in the emergency room ? did the emergency room staff gi- the emergency room staff give Hannah some iv fluids and things like iv fluids ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: yeah . the emergency room staff gave Hannah some fluids and the emergency room staff told Hannah just to try to really cut out eating any extra sweets and get back into Hannah's normal routine and diet, but Hannah is still staying with Hannah's family, and Hannah's mom and Hannah's grandmother are big bakers so Hannah thinks cutting out eating any extra sweets will be really hard to get into a routine there so Hannah just wants to make sure Hannah is nipping the high blood sugar thing in the bud.

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay . and the doctor saw Hannah went through a review of systems sheet with a nurse . the doctor just wants to ask Hannah a few more questions from that standpoint . so has Hannah had any fever or chills ?

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: any chest pain or shortness of breath ?

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: any joint pain or muscle aches ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: Hannah does have osteoarthritis though , so the osteoarthritis , when Hannah says , " no , " the osteoarthritis is not , like , nothing out of the norm-

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: perfect . um , and then , the doctor knows that Hannah has this history of a-fib , atrial fibrillation . has Hannah felt Hannah's heart racing at all , recently ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: only when Hannah is really anxious . Hannah , Hannah feels Hannah's heart , um , pumping a little bit . but other than that Hannah has n't .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay . all right . and how is Hannah doing with Hannah's reflux ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: Hannah has been doing well . Hannah has been taking the medication that the doctor prescribed to Hannah and the medication has been helping a lot .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay , yeah . the doctor sees here Hannah is on the protonix , 40 milligrams , once a day .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: so that's , is Hannah doing well with protonix ?

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay . all right . so let's go ahead and do a quick physical exam . so , listening to Hannah , um , look , or looking at Hannah , Hannah appears in no distress . Hannah's thyroid is a little enlarged , but non-tender . there's no carotid bruits , Hannah's heart is in irregular rate and rhythm , and Hannah's lungs are clear . Hannah's abdomen is nice and soft , and Hannah has no edema in Hannah's lower extremities . let's go ahead and look at some of Hannah's test results .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: hey , dragon ? show the doctor the blood sugar . hey , dragon ? show the doctor the blood glucose . hey , dragon ? show the doctor the hemoglobin a1c . okay , so the doctor sees here that both Hannah's blood sugar and Hannah's hemoglobin a1c are elevated . so Hannah's blood sugars have probably been running high for probably a few months . okay ? um , let's go ahead and look at some of Hannah's other results . hey , dragon ? show the doctor the diabetes labs . yeah . okay . yeah , so , um , the doctor's impression of Hannah at this time , um , for Hannah's high blood sugars , that Hannah does have this diagnosis of diabetes . now , the doctor and Hannah are not sure if the diabetes is type i or type ii . even patients in , you know , in their mid-twenties can develop type i diabetes , so the doctor and Hannah are gon na go off and , the doctor and Hannah are gon na go ahead and , and send some labs to work the diabetes type up . hey , dragon ? order a complete metabolic panel . and the doctor would like to start Hannah on metformin , 500 milligrams , twice a day . metformin will help keep Hannah's blood sugars down .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: hey , dragon ? order metformin , 500 milligrams , twice a day . and then if some of the autoimmune labs that come back show that Hannah has an autoimmune disorder , meaning that Hannah would have type i diabetes , then the doctor and Hannah would have to go ahead and put Hannah on insulin . but the doctor is gon na hold off until the doctor has those results , okay ?

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: but in the meantime , the doctor really wants Hannah to avoid , uh , foods high in sugar . um , and the nurse will come in and the nurse will give Hannah a glucometer and teach Hannah how to do blood sugar testing . okay ?

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: and then , from an atrial fibrillation standpoint , Hannah's heart , even though Hannah is in atrial fibrillation right now , is in a good rate . and the doctor will just continue Hannah on Hannah's metoprolol , 25 milligrams , twice a day . does Hannah need a refill on metoprolol ?

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: hey , dragon ? order a refill on metoprolol , 25 milligrams , twice a day . and for Hannah's reflux , the doctor will just continue Hannah on the protonix , okay ?

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: does Hannah have any questions , hannah ?

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay , so the nurse will come in and the nurse will go over all that stuff with Hannah .
